name: QuickBooks Online API
what_it_does: Intuit's QuickBooks Online REST API for managing accounts, invoices, customers, vendors, expenses, and financial reports for small businesses. The dominant small business accounting API in North America.
use_cases: Automating invoice creation and payment tracking from agent workflows
Syncing customer and vendor data between CRM and accounting systems
Pulling financial reports (P&L, balance sheet) for AI-driven analysis
Automating expense categorization and journal entries
Building accounting automation for small business owners
not_for: Enterprise accounting (SAP, Oracle — not QuickBooks scale)
best_when: An agent needs to automate accounting tasks for small to mid-size businesses already using QuickBooks Online, especially invoice and expense management.
avoid_when: Your customers use enterprise ERP systems, non-Intuit accounting software, or are outside North America where QBO adoption is low.

auth.notes: OAuth2 only — no API keys. Requires full OAuth2 code flow with user authorization. Access tokens expire in 1 hour, refresh tokens in 100 days. Must redirect users through Intuit OAuth. Scopes: com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting, com.intuit.quickbooks.payment.

pricing.paid_tiers: QuickBooks Online Simple Start $35/month, Plus $65/month, Advanced $100/month
pricing.notes: API access requires active QuickBooks Online subscription for the business being accessed. Developer sandbox is free. No separate API billing — QBO subscription covers it.

performance.rate_limits: 500 requests per minute per OAuth token, throttled during peak hours

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include fault code and detail message. Messages are often technical and not agent-friendly. Validation errors sometimes reference internal field names. Documentation and errors don't always align.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No formal idempotency keys. Some objects can be created with SyncToken for conflict detection. Agents must check for existing records before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 is mandatory — no API keys — requires user authorization flow which is hard for autonomous agents
SyncToken is required on all updates — must fetch current SyncToken before any write
Minor versions (minorversion query param) unlock features but break compatibility — pin to specific version
Sandbox and production use different company IDs — easy to mix up
Rate limit throttling is aggressive during tax season and business hours
DELETE is not supported — use void or archive operations instead
Query language is custom SQL-like but has significant limitations
